LEGAL & COMPLIANCE GUIDE

Scaffolding Permits

Understand when you need a license for your scaffolding and who is responsible for obtaining it.

When is a License Required?

The general rule is: **If any part of the scaffolding is placed on a public highway (pavement, road, or alleyway), a license is required.** If the scaffolding is entirely on your private land, you usually do not need a permit.

Project Scenarios:

  • 🏠 **Private Front Garden**: No license required.
  • 🛣️ **Public Pavement**: License required from local council.
  • 🏢 **Restricted Access Road**: Specific traffic management may be needed.
  • 📍 **Heritage/Listed building**: High-level permissions might apply.

Who applies for it?

In almost all cases, **the scaffolding company must apply for the license**, not the homeowner or the builder. We have the required public liability insurance (£10M+) that councils demand before granting a permit.

Council & Permit Factors

Cost of Permits:

Council fees vary but generally range from **£50 to £200 per month** in London. These are fixed by the local authority and passed on directly to the client.

Approval Time:

Most London councils require at least **7-14 working days** of notice. Some offer 'fast-track' services for an additional fee.

Safety Requirements:

Councils often mandate the use of foam padding, warning lights, and high-visibility taping on all public-facing scaffolds.

Let us handle the Paperwork

When you hire SWP Scaffolding, we take care of the entire council application process for your project in London & Surrey.
